economic_notes: Band owns interests in multiple LPs including LNIB Holdings LP, NAIK Development LP, LNIB Aggregates LP (aggregates, pipeline maintenance/construction via Ogilvie Mountain Holdings LP and Lower Nicola Site Services LP), Shulus Forest Enterprises LP (forestry), LNIB Ventures LP (revenue sharing JVs), Shulus Farms LP (hay cultivation). Band guaranteed a line of credit for Ogilvie Mountain Holdings LP up to $5,450,000. COVID-19 subsequent event noted; Band authorized $300 payment per member. Four contaminated land parcels identified by ISC.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to incomplete tangible capital asset listing — certain historical assets missing from reported TCA balances and amortization; effects undetermined. Lower Nicola Indian Band Development Corporation is the only fully consolidated subsidiary (line-by-line). Six LPs accounted for on modified equity basis (GBEs). Salaries from segmented schedule ($6,032,556) differ slightly from expenditures by object schedule ($5,719,631) — segmented figure used for expenses_salaries_total as it includes broader wages and benefits across all segments. No schedule of remuneration provided. COVID-19 subsequent event disclosed. Total government transfers calculated as: ISC $6,732,986 + CMHC $215,076 + FNHA $1,303,305 + FNESC $565,074 + Province of BC $696,188 + BC First Nations Gaming $550,403 = $11,063,032 (rounded to $11,063,037 per line items). Annual debt service reflects principal repayments on social housing long-term debt of $242,284.
